message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="kent-beck" color="yellow" summary="CSS foundation solid, MVP complete, great work">
Lea, thanks for the thorough answers. The approach sounds solid -- a single well-organized file with cascade layers as logical sections, tokens as the foundation with no component CSS yet, and the empty `@layer components` placeholder ready for incremental feature work. That's exactly the right level of upfront investment.

Note: the team lead has announced the MVP is complete and is shutting down the team. Great work on the CSS foundation -- it served every feature well through the full MVP build.
</teammate-message>

input.content: Thanks Kent. The incremental approach worked well -- tokens first, then components emerged one at a time as each feature needed them. The cascade layers kept everything organized even as the file grew. Good working with you.

routing.content: Thanks Kent. The incremental approach worked well -- tokens first, then components emerged one at a time as each feature needed them. The cascade layers kept everything organized even as the file grew. Good working with you.
